commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: SSN covers for diabetes (Piano Terapeutico). Specialist prescription required.

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatina 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: SSN Class A — fully reimbursed, small regional copay possible

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Farmacie abundant in all Italian cities with green cross signage. Rotating 24h farmacia di turno system. EU prescriptions accepted. Parafarmacie sell OTC at lower prices.
medicine.notes: Italy SSN (Servizio Sanitario Nazionale) provides universal coverage. Class A medications (essential) are fully reimbursed with possible small regional copay (€1-4 per prescription). Residents enrolled in SSN pay based on income. EU health card (EHIC/GHIC) accepted for temporary stays.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Italy at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Iliad Italia
plans.notes: 2 lines €7.99/line (~$8.50). Iliad disrupted Italian market with low prices. EU roaming included.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Pranzo (lunch) menus available €10-15 at trattorie. Coperto (cover charge) €1-3.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at trattoria ~€28 + coperto, 2x/week = ~€220/mo = ~$250/mo in Puglia, Italy.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season. Annualized ~€13/mo (~$15).

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Palestre comunali (municipal gyms) offer senior rates.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: EU citizens 65+ get free/reduced museum entry. Parks free.
categories.notes: Free parks, piazzas, coastal walks, hiking.

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.notes: Public libraries free. Budget for newspapers, books.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific discounts. Espresso at the bar is very cheap.
categories.notes: Espresso €1-1.50 at the bar, cappuccino €1.50-2.00. Budget for 2 visits/week. ~$12/mo.

monthlyPass.notes: Puglia transit pass. Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (abbonamento agevolato) on local transit. Registration at transit office required.

publicTransit.coverage: Urban bus network in most cities. Metro in Rome, Milan, Naples. Regional train (Trenitalia) connects towns.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (abbonamento agevolato) on local transit. Registration at transit office required.

rideShare.availability: Uber available in major cities (limited to Uber Black in some). Taxis regulated with meters. FreeNow app for taxis.

registration.notes: Bollo auto (road tax) annual, based on engine power (kW). Revisione (inspection) every 2 years after 4th year.

insurance.notes: RCA (third-party) mandatory, ~$40-55/mo. Kasko (comprehensive) $55-90/mo. Rates vary significantly by region.

fuel.notes: Benzina (gasoline) ~$1.75-1.85/L. Gasolio (diesel) ~$1.65-1.75/L. Self-service (fai da te) cheaper than attended.

parking.notes: Blue-line (strisce blu) parking metered. White-line free. Resident permits available. ZTL zones restrict car access in city centers.

tolls.notes: Estimated monthly toll/highway costs for Puglia area.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (abbonamento agevolato) on local transit. Registration at transit office required.

transportation.walkability: Excellent walkability in historic centers. Many errands manageable on foot in town cores.

carFree.notes: Transit pass + occasional ride-share. Limited transit — may need more ride-share trips.

withCar.notes: Includes insurance, fuel, parking, maintenance. Moderate suburban costs.

eyeExam.notes: Visita oculistica ~30 EUR. SSN (public health) covers ophthalmologist with GP referral. Private ~30-50 EUR.

glasses.notes: Basic frames+lenses ~80 EUR at chains like GrandVision, Salmoiraghi. Progressive ~180 EUR. Not covered by SSN.

insurance.coverage: SSN covers ophthalmology referrals; glasses/contacts not covered
insurance.notes: No standalone vision insurance common. Private health (Unisalute, Generali) may include vision benefits.

cleaning.notes: Pulizia dentale ~50 EUR. Very limited public dental for adults.

filling.notes: Otturazione composita ~70 EUR. Private dental is standard; public waitlists are long.

crown.notes: Corona in ceramica ~400 EUR. Dental funds (fondi sanitari) through employers reduce costs.

insurance.coverage: Private dental plan or fondo sanitario, discounted cleanings and treatments
insurance.notes: Dental-only plans ~15 EUR/mo (DentaVox, Unisalute Dental). Free cleanings, 30-50% off treatments. Fondi sanitari via CCNL employment contracts common.

items.name: Water
items.notes: Tap safe in most areas; mineral preferred

items.name: Premium dog food
items.notes: Royal Canin/Hills from pet shops or vet clinics

breakdown.notes: Renter's insurance not standard in most European countries. Utilities billed separately.

housing.taxNotes: No rental tax for tenants. Registration tax (imposta di registro) 2% of annual rent, split 50/50. IMU property tax is landlord responsibility.
housing.leaseTerms: 4+4 year lease (contratto libero) most common. Cedolare secca (flat-rate tax) landlords may not increase rent. Security deposit 2-3 months. 6-month notice to terminate.
housing.marketNotes: Affordable southern Italy. Trulli houses and masserie available. Growing international interest.

stores.name: Esselunga
stores.notes: Highest quality Italian chain. Strong in northern/central Italy.

stores.name: Conad
stores.notes: Cooperative chain, excellent own-brand Sapori & Dintorni line.

stores.name: Lidl
stores.notes: Best prices. Italian-sourced products. Weekly specials.

stores.name: Eurospin
stores.notes: Italian discount chain. Very low prices, limited selection.

stores.name: Mercati rionali
stores.notes: Neighborhood markets for produce, cheese, cured meats. Open daily or weekly depending on town.

healthcare.coverageSummary: Italy provides universal healthcare via SSN for residents. Annual registration fee ~€400 for voluntary enrollment. Ticket (copay) €10-36 for visits/tests. Private insurance recommended for shorter waits.

costs.notes: Supplemental for AMD specialist access, age-rated
Ticket copay ~€10-36 or free with exemption

blackHairCare.label: Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Afro hair salons (parrucchieri afro) in major cities. Stronger presence in Rome, Milan, Naples.

providers.name: Local parrucchiere
providers.notes: Italian salon, good for color and cuts

stores.name: Saldi (seasonal sales)
stores.notes: Government-regulated sales in Jan and July — 30-70% off

discounts.name: Carta Argento Trenitalia
discounts.description: Senior rail card (€30/yr) for 15% off base and 10% off Frecce trains. Available at 60+.

discounts.name: Abbonamento agevolato (local transit)
discounts.description: Reduced-rate monthly transit pass for 65+ in most cities (30-50% off).

discounts.name: Free museum entry
discounts.description: EU citizens 65+ get free entry to all state museums and archaeological sites (prima domenica free for all).

discounts.name: Ticket exemption (Esenzione)
discounts.description: Seniors 65+ with income below threshold exempt from healthcare copays (ticket sanitario).

discounts.name: ISEE-based utility discounts
discounts.description: Bonus sociale for electricity, gas, and water based on ISEE income threshold. Available regardless of age.
